-->
# SOD: yyyy-mm-dd<-N if not session 1>

> **Strict format.** Generated by Code at start of a work session, derived from the prior EOD plus the issue tracker. Do not add, remove, or reorder sections. GM uses this to plan today's prompts.

## Header
- **Date:** yyyy-mm-dd
- **Session number:** N (1 for the first SOD on this calendar date, 2 if continuing later same day, etc.)
- **Filename:** sod.md (session 1) or sod-N.md (later sessions)
- **Day index:** N (work-session counter since DevFlow started — does not reset for second-session same-day SODs)
- **Prior EOD:** path to the eod file this SOD was built from, or "none — first day"
- **Generated at:** yyyy-mm-dd HH:MM

## 1. Carryover from prior EOD
Pulled directly from the prior EOD's "Tomorrow's seed" plus anything left in-progress or blocked. The "prior EOD" is the highest-numbered eod file from the most recent prior date folder, or earlier today if a session already closed.

- **In progress:** items started but not finished, with status
- **Blocked:** items still blocked, with the blocker
- **Seed text:** the literal seed text from the prior EOD

## 2. Issue tracker snapshot
Pulled from docs/roadmap.md (refreshed at this SOD from the live tracker).

- **High priority open:** count and one-line list (max 5)
- **Medium priority open:** count only
- **Recently opened (last 3 days):** count and one-line list (max 3)
- **Stale issues (>30 days no activity):** count — flag if growing

## 3. Five-day pipeline
This is the working plan. Rolling window — "Day +N" means Nth future work session, not calendar day.

### Today (Day 0)
Items committed for today. Each item is scoped tight enough to fit in one or two prompts.

| # | Item | Source | Estimate | Scope notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <one-line description> | <issue ID / EOD seed / GM directive> | <S/M/L> | <in-scope summary> |
| 2 | ... | ... | ... | ... |

S = single prompt, M = 2-3 prompts, L = needs to be broken into smaller items before starting.

### Day +1 to +2 — committed next
Items the project commits to working on next, blockers permitting. Do not list more than what fits in 2 work sessions.

| # | Item | Source | Estimate | Blocker if any |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ... | ... | ... | ... |

### Day +3 to +5 — planned, lower confidence
Items planned but expected to shift. Limit to 5 items — anything beyond goes to backlog.

| # | Item | Source | Estimate |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ... | ... | ... |

### Beyond +5 — backlog reference
Single bullet pointing at the issue tracker. Do not list items here. Example:
- See issue tracker for backlog. Last reviewed yyyy-mm-dd.

## 4. Today's structured plan
GM uses this as the basis for today's prompts. Each plan entry maps to one or more Today items.

### Plan entry 1
- **Maps to today item:** #<number from Today table>
- **Objective:** one sentence — what success looks like
- **In scope:** bullets
- **Out of scope:** bullets — things to explicitly not touch
- **Success criteria:** how we know it's done (specific, testable)
- **Dependencies:** other plan entries that must finish first, or "none"
- **Phase plan:** which of the 7 steps need extra attention (e.g., "Explore step needs to map auth flow before planning", "Step 4 diff-review will trigger because of schema change")

### Plan entry 2
... (repeat structure)

## 5. Risks and watchouts for today
- **Risks:** what could derail today's plan
- **Decisions GM needs from user:** items that need user input before Code can proceed
- **Cowork checks queued:** UI verifications already known to be needed today

## 6. Code's notes for GM
Anything Code observed about codebase health, drift, or fragile areas relevant to today's plan. Brief.

- ...

---

## Format rules to enforce

- **Today section** must have between 1 and 4 items. If more than 4 are needed, push some to Day +1-2.
- **Day +1 to +2** must have at most 6 items total.
- **Day +3 to +5** must have at most 5 items total.
- Every Today item must have at least one matching Plan entry in section 4.
- Every Plan entry must have In scope AND Out of scope filled in (Out of scope can be "none specifically excluded — standard scope discipline applies").
- If section 1 carryover is non-empty, those items must appear in section 3 Today or Day +1-2 — they cannot vanish.
